APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TERRENCE J. COLEMAN, Esq. of Pillsbury & Coleman, LLP (Tel: 415-433-8000) for Plaintiff Mary Neagle;
Atty. RANDI PERRY SPALLINA, Esq. of Bressler, Amery & Ross (Tel: 925-856-6251) for Defendants Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith Incorporated and Richard L. Mazur.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED AND RICHARD MAZUR'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay The Judicial Proceeding.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

"Defendants' petition to compel arbitration and stay the judicial proceeding" is denied. The 95-year-old plaintiff alleges that defendants - who oversaw both her securities accounts and insurance needs - allowed her long-term care insurance policy to lapse. Defendants point to arbitration provisions in three agreements. The first is for a "cash management account" not involving insurance. (Michal Dec. Ex. A.) Plaintiff signed the other two agreements as "trustee" for trusts (Id. at Exs. B, C), and her long-term care insurance was individual, not involving the trusts. Thus, defendants failed to carry their burden to prove the existence of a relevant arbitration agreement (Sandoval-Ryan v. Oleander Holdings LLC (2020) 58 Cal.App.5th 217, 222), and plaintiff's other arguments need not be reached.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
